index.html.title: index
index.html.description:
Ember Deck is a project still in early development, so this site may update and look unrecognisable after the fact!Said plainly, Ember Deck is a TTRPG Deckbuilder made for (ideally) 3-4 Player groups, but it should still scale relatively well until about 5-6 people. It was an idea heavily inspired by gems such as "Slay The Spire", "Hearthstone", "Slice and Dice", and "Morimens". The gameplay features tight resource management in a low-math, high-keyword environment, giving a couple of anchor points to make your own character and their deck unique and powerful, whereas the Setting provides plenty of opportunity for "angsty" or "against all odds" roleplay in a grimdark world, where magic is ubiquitous, but hope is scant.Once, there was HELL. Every day, souls passed in and out, getting incinerated and sent to the Hall of Echoes, pure. One day an apocalyptic event happened. Because of that, HELL is working overtime and is still centuries behind. Because of that, Souls get clogged up in the in-between, and that radiation is called magic, which seeps into the living. Until finally, their bodies can't take it anymore, and burn up from the inside.*The LORE dropdown is a good place to start for anyone curious about the world and the gritty reality found therein, ex.: VIBE. For the Rules Lawyers: here's the short end of it, as the detailed rulings fall under their own dropdowns, labelled BS, DS, or CHARACTER.Once a concept of who you'd like to play as forms in your mind, the preferable place to go is the CHARACTER>OATHS Section, where you'll find all three major Oaths (which function like classes, and determine your starting Attributes - Prisms), these in turn determine your Starting Deck.
All Oaths start with 10 BACKBONE cards making up their deck, but the keywords may vary based on chosen Oath and even subtle differences between the same Oath's decks are possible at times.
Now, having a concept, embodied by an Oath, and a Deck, you select your BURST, and your PASSIVES (Each Oath offers a different selection). These are the anchor points mentioned before - they'll define your playstyle heavily. Once that's in order, and your friends have done the same, you're set! (Don't forget to send at least 4 sentences worth of a backstory to your GM, though.)As with most RPGs, the weight of this point lands mostly on the GM, however some things to keep in mind as a Player skipping through sections: Checks are made with d6's, many of them at times (up to 8-ish). Miniatures are welcome but leave the rulers at home - combat doesn't use movement. Agree on a vision you and the other players have on your bond, how you met and connected, it will save the GM a couple hair-pulls. Enjoy! Changed Keyword function and Naming:
Empower -> Momentum.
Empower was Changed to provide a readily accessible pool instead of a small trickle.
Brace was updated to mirror this change.
Focus -> Empower.
Ward now scales with Insight and prevents enemy keywords as well as statuses.
Delay was Removed.
Added IMAGES sections across the vault to provide visuals.
CASE STUDY was removed for cleanup Certain notes have received related images inside the notes
CINDERS, and, HEARTHS AND EMBERS were moved to the FRACTIONS dropdown
The SYNC-AXIS is now more firmly tied to combat through the updated EMPOWERand BRACE keywords
BURST note was corrected, showing an improved formula for post-burst draw calculation
INVENTORY section was brushed up, adding needed specifications and removing unnecessary repetitions. Allowed Curios to have limited Mechanical value.
Clarified PIERCE's scaling
Removed false keyword scalings from GUILE, INSIGHT, and MIGHT
Clarified SHARDS in-combat effects, and removed Delay from the list.
Added the EXPLORATION section
Added Several Notes explaining the rules of EXPLORATION (NODE - RESTNODE - PATHNODE - UNKNOWNNODE - UNSTABLENODE - ENCOUNTER, and REFERENCE IMAGE)
Expanded the TURNS page with an infographic, which can also be found in ENEMIES>RULINGS
Added HEARTH SHEET as well as EMBER SHEET
Compiled existing notes into a single file INFECTION
Streamlined LIGHTNING
Made the HOLLOW effect more inconvenient Changed the Oath of the Crucible to Oath of the Pyre for easier differentiation and recognition from Conduit
Introduced Party-influenced Status cards added when taking DMG at 0 VITALS and reshuffling the shared deck during prolonged combats
Added the DECK note for clarifications of card handling
Expanded on the Tools and Curios section within INVENTORY
Altered CHARACTER/OATHS/MISTWALKER/PASSIVES to DISCARDthe first status instead of resisting an enemy intent for wider versatility
Created Intent Loops for Firebomber.canvas and Cultist.canvas and deleted Cinder Goliath.png
Also added a short guide to reading Intent loops in the RULINGS section
Also in the RULINGS section, introduced a potentially volatile mechanic of Front and Back row enemies. To be tested.
Clarified interactions between MOMENTUM and AREA and LIGHTNING
Redefined OATH_OF_THE_PYRE CHARACTER/OATHS/PYRE/BURSTS to not rely on MOMENTUM so heavily, changing one entirely to propose a new playstyle
Added a third option for handling hitting 0 Vitals.
Changed SHARDS in-combat complexity Added several new Enemies, and expanded upon the existing ones
Changed Reshuffle rules: The Shared DECK now keeps the reshuffle count between combats and resets at rest sites only.
WARD was removed. Rest sites are now explicitly Reusable.
NODE - UNKNOWN now has a roll table of effects.
GUARD's effects can synergise with certain Passives and Trinkets
ECHO was removed.
Added suggested Encounter Templates to the RULINGS tab.
Added a survivability QoL for Enemies in the form of Innate Stacking GUARD
IGNITE has been refreshed to keep it interesting and Viable
Changed BLEED to scale with MIGHT to keep relative balance with each Prism now having 3 Primary keywords each.
Updated BLEED to be more interactive, bringing it up to speed with the previously tweaked IGNITE
Prepared new Ember and Hearth Sheets for the next Playtest down the line. They can be found in the EMBER SHEET and HEARTH SHEET notes.
Added a new STATUS - INFECTION which balances out the improved VAMPIRIC and High-MEND parties.
Updated LEVEL_UP to include proper Node names as well as include the conditional for EXHAUST retrieval, and clearing up Adding new cards
EXHAUST has been explained in more detail.
Changed the odds for NODE - UNKNOWN so it can no longer trigger an NPC/Lore event, and instead is empty more often
Added newly designed, visually polished improvements for the EMBER SHEET and HEARTH SHEET. Moved the info about Prisms to the Character dropdown. Updated Prisms section with accurate scaling keywords.
Temporarily removed MERCILESS.
Improved upon the design and numbers on some Enemies, also added the Trench Horror (Hunter).canvas, as well as a dropdown with Encounter Templates, ranging from Basic through Difficult all the way to Hard.
Made TREMBLING more dangerous by increasing its Cost to play.
Made MIST less threatening by delaying its activation.
Changed Merciless to apply to ANY targettable card (Strike, Mend, Bleed, Ignite), but also to scale with the player's highest Prism, lessening MIGHT's dominance.
Tweaked starting Oath decks, balancing the Empower/Mend pivot for OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT, and tossing in a starting MOMENTUM for the OATH_OF_THE_PYRE, to allow easier tasting of the other (Non- STRIKE/GUARD) keywords.
Removed the Oath-Only-new card restriction from the LEVEL_UP options
Split up the pages for TOOLS, CURIOS, and ARTEFACTS from the INVENTORY section.
Introduced a pilot mechanic called Madness, which is a result of exploring the MIST without proper equipment. Stabilised the rulings regarding item bonuses and applications so players and GMs alike can make more informed decisions about their equipment.
Added strictness to CURIOS rulings so they don't overlap as much with ARTEFACTS and instead fill their own role, one card at a time.
More examples of CURIOS and ARTEFACTS effects.
Clarified the wording on BS ROLLS (CHECKS).
Moved around help text on the LEVEL_UP tab.
Removed the EXHAUST mention from SHARDS as to avoid repetition.
Removed the "Affects ALL allies" text from AREA, as the players' stats and buffs/debuffs are shared anyway.
Improved MERCILESS scaling to reward target picking.
Improved PIERCE by removing its scaling.
Elaborated on the EXHAUST impact on deckbuilding.
Rephrased the INJURY wording to match the keywords used throughout the system.
Nerfed the OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT passive that allowed the rest of the party to ignore their INSIGHT scores completely.
Narrowed down OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER passive that discarded drawn STATUS cards.
Specified the N in OATH_OF_THE_PYRE Burst page meant Player count, to match the system wording.
Specified the trigger condition for PASSIVES.
Big BURST Rework, removing the ENERGY cost, introducing infinite Charge accumulation, and bonuses tied to it, as well as gentler next-turn-draw-penalty rules. Uploaded Images to the Character Section, to help visualise various playable archetypes.
Added ominous three dots "..." in the DECK page.
Added a rule of thumb to the SYNC-AXIS page.
Removed the outdated combat tracker from the TURNS page.
Added a description to the WAR page.
Separated pages for HORDE-SOCIETY, MAGIC-SOCIETY, DIESEL-SOCIETY, CLASSICAL-SOCIETY and a GM_Guide for deepening them. Names TBD.
Added the REDACTED tag to any pages in need of large edits.
Added NODE - PORTAL, NODE - DOOR, and NODE - REWARD as they appeared on REFERENCE IMAGE but were not present in the rules.
Created the CHARACTER CREATION page. It is empty.
Polished and released a beta for the TALK_THE_TALK. Introduced NPC Quirks in a specialised Table.
Conceptualised and added the Gotcha minigame.
Introduced a new faction - NOMAD-SOCIETY.
Added a variation of the NODE - REWARD, NODE - RITUAL. Attempted to make combat more dynamic by introducing a slightly altered layout, and set phases to de-complicate tracking.
Formalised combat rewards in the SHARDS page.
Altered Backbone Purist as an archetype, bringing it to speed with the other styles of play. BACKBONE
Removed the ECONOMICS and CULTURE pages as they were left empty long enough for me to develop Societies with their own takes on these issues, see NOMAD-SOCIETY, MAGIC-SOCIETY, HORDE-SOCIETY, DIESEL-SOCIETY, CLASSICAL-SOCIETY for more.
Updated the index page itself, adding further rules briefing. Briefly lost my mind as the newest deployments stopped showing patch notes after 0.7.3

headers.heading: Welcome to the official Ember Deck Vault!

bs-(between-stabbings)/bs-rolls-(checks).title: BS ROLLS (CHECKS)
bs-(between-stabbings)/bs-rolls-(checks).description:
Used to resolve arguments or environmental obstacles. Pool: 2d6 Base + Prism X d6. Success: Each die showing 4, 5, or 6. Sixes count as double.
Failure: Each die showing 3, 2, or 1.
Difficulty: DC 1-2: Standard task. DC 3: Strenuous. DC 4: Dangerous/Elite. DC 6+: Near-impossible.

bs-(between-stabbings)/level_up.html.title: LEVEL_UP
bs-(between-stabbings)/level_up.html.description:
Level-Ups occur on NODE - REST/between EXPLORATIONs, and use SHARDS accumulated during travels, which allow for: Increasing a single Prism's Value (-5 shards)
Removing Cards (-3 shards) *A player’s deck must always contain at least 4 BACKBONE Cards.
Adding Keywords to Cards (-3 shards for Primary, -2 shards for Secondary and Tertiary, -1 for CURSES) When adding a Keyword to a card, you may (only) choose a Keyword that scales with a Prism you have at least 1 point in.
Purging permanent STATUS cards (-3 shards)
Adding new BACKBONE Cards (-2 shards) *When adding a new Card, it is encouraged to follow the New Keyword rule from above, unless you are planning several steps ahead of course. Changing Keywords already on Cards (-2)
Restoring EXHAUSTed cards (-1) Removing Keywords from Cards (+1)

bs-(between-stabbings)/shards.html.title: SHARDS
bs-(between-stabbings)/shards.html.description:
Shards are the universal currency of the Hearth. They act as both long-term progression and can be crushed as mid-combat consumables: Cost: 0 ENERGY.
Effect: grant +1 ENERGY AND +1 DRAW Crushed Shards are gone forever and cannot be used for progression.
MIGHT = enemies defeated. 1 enemy kill = 1 Shard/10 HP the enemy had. INSIGHT = full intent loops realized (understanding the enemy). 1 FULL intent Loop realised = 3 Shard. GUILE = fewer turns taken / efficiency. Baseline Turns (=2x Enemy count - N (Player count)). Baseline Turns - Turns Taken = 2 Shards each. Thus the rewards push a “pick 2 of 3” shape: get out of there fast, watch and learn, You can’t get both every fight.

character/cards/keywords/primary/brace.h.title: BRACE
character/cards/keywords/primary/brace.h.description:
Pool of Value. When gaining GUARD you may expend any amount of stacks from the pool and add it to the total GUARD received. Pool decreases when HP damage is taken.
Depending on the SYNC-AXIS; Harmony - halve, rounding up. Discord - remove all stacks.
Scales with GUILE

character/cards/keywords/primary/counter.title: COUNTER
character/cards/keywords/primary/counter.description: Grants Value stacks. Deals Value DMG back when hit; halve stacks (round up) after each trigger. Scales with GUILE

character/cards/keywords/primary/empower.title: EMPOWER
character/cards/keywords/primary/empower.description: Adds Value to the numeric effect of the next card played. Scales with INSIGHT

character/cards/keywords/primary/guard.h.title: GUARD
character/cards/keywords/primary/guard.h.description: Grants Value Block - stopping incoming DMG. Scales with GUILE

character/cards/keywords/primary/mend.ht.title: MEND
character/cards/keywords/primary/mend.ht.description:
Restores Value Vitality. Can remove BLEED stacks before they deal damage.
Scales with INSIGHT

character/cards/keywords/primary/strike..title: STRIKE
character/cards/keywords/primary/strike..description: Deal Value DMG. Scales with MIGHT

character/cards/keywords/secondary/light.title: LIGHTNING
character/cards/keywords/secondary/light.description: Hits two targets: One with the lowest and one with the highest HP. In case of only one target available hits it twice.

character/cards/keywords/secondary/merci.title: MERCILESS
character/cards/keywords/secondary/merci.description:
If target has no Block, add 2xValue to the card's total Value.
Scales with Your highest Prism.

character/cards/keywords/secondary/vampi.title: VAMPIRIC
character/cards/keywords/secondary/vampi.description: Restore VITALS equal to 1/2 Value when hitting HP. Stacks.

character/cards/status_cards/doubt.html.title: DOUBT
character/cards/status_cards/doubt.html.description: RETAIN At the end of turn, lose 1 BURSTcharge. UNPLAYABLE

character/cards/status_cards/injury.html.title: INJURY
character/cards/status_cards/injury.html.description: Cost: 2 Energy. RETAINEXHAUST. While in hand, your BURST is UNPLAYABLE.

character/cards/status_cards/panic.html.title: PANIC
character/cards/status_cards/panic.html.description: Cost: 1 Energy. When drawn, DISCARD another card from your hand. It does not contribute to Burst Gauge when discarded this way. RETAIN

character/cards/status_cards/trembling.h.title: TREMBLING
character/cards/status_cards/trembling.h.description: Cost: 2 Energy. EXHAUST. While in hand, playing another card deals Self DMG = ENERGY cost of the card played.

character/cards/status_cards/wound.html.title: WOUND
character/cards/status_cards/wound.html.description: When drawn, take 2 PIERCE DMG. UNPLAYABLE

character/cards/backbone.html.title: BACKBONE
character/cards/backbone.html.description:
Starter Cards with only One Keyword, they have an innate +3 Value. Backbone cards are limited to the single-keyword cards provided in the Starting Oath Deck. Adding any keyword or modifying them turns them into Custom cards, removing the +3 bonus. Likewise, removing extra Keywords from a CUSTOMS card brings it back to a Backbone once its left with just One Keyword. Deck Rule: A player’s deck must always contain at least 4 Backbone Cards, as detailed below. Backbones lose 1 point of their bonus per Rest or Level-Up.
Oaths of permanence: at character creation, the player chooses four cards that are locked in as their signature core, these Backbone cards will never Decay through rests, but can also never be removed or altered via SHARDS. ARTEFACTS and CURIOS still apply. That gives the Players: a stable identity, a way to keep some Backbone value permanently, Any other Backbone cards can still decay, evolve, or be replaced over time. That gives “purist” a clear meaning: not “never change anything,” but “maintain a tight, trusted core and avoid experimental drift.” It also creates a nice tension between stability and fresh power. A simple version could be: Backbones decay over a couple of rests. A decayed Backbone can be removed for a shard cost. The player may then buy a replacement Backbone. The new one starts at full bonus. That creates an active deck-maintenance economy without making decay feel purely punitive.

character/cards/customs.html.title: CUSTOMS
character/cards/customs.html.description:
Custom Cards have more than One Keyword. Can be UNIQUE or upgraded from BACKBONE cards.
Card ENERGY Cost Scales with Keyword count (1:1), making complex cards more expensive to play.
They use only the related Prism's Value for effects, where applicable, without the BACKBONE Reliability Bonus.

character/cards/status.html.title: STATUS
character/cards/status.html.description:
STATUS cards are Negative effects applied by Enemies, Events, or through losing VITALS. Unless noted otherwise (by having their ENERGY cost listed), these are UNPLAYABLE, and disappear at the end of combat.
STATUS cards are ownerless, and so are dealt out randomly from left to right to players with the least amount of STATUS cards in hand.

character/cards/unique.html.title: UNIQUE
character/cards/unique.html.description: Unique Cards are ones gained through Backstory, or Campaign Events, usually crafted by the GM, or in collaboration with the player.

character/oaths/conduit/bursts.html.title: BURSTS
character/oaths/conduit/bursts.html.description:
Enemy loses all GUARD and COUNTER stacks.
STRIKE LIGHTNING BLEED
Play up to INSIGHT cards for free, if they scale with INSIGHT (from other players too).
IGNITE 2 AREA. Already IGNITEd enemies take their total IGNITE stacks as DMG immediately (Does not remove stacks).

character/oaths/conduit/oath_of_the_cond.title: OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT
character/oaths/conduit/oath_of_the_cond.description:
Your Prism scores are as follows: 0 MIGHT
1 GUILE
2 INSIGHT You may allocate a single point to a chosen Prism. Your Starting deck consists of:
3x STRIKE
3x GUARD
2x EMPOWER
2x MEND

character/oaths/conduit/passives.html.title: PASSIVES
character/oaths/conduit/passives.html.description:
Leftover GUARD is halved at turn end, instead of vanishing completely.
Your IGNITE effects deal damage at the start of the enemy's turn.
Allies can use Your INSIGHT if its higher than theirs, when playing an INSIGHT-scaling card, once per turn.
EMPOWER INSIGHT the first card played each turn.

character/oaths/mistwalker/bursts.html.title: BURSTS
character/oaths/mistwalker/bursts.html.description: Reduce the cost of all cards currently dealt to 1 this turn. Next card played this turn takes effect 2 extra times (so 3 activations total). Stun an enemy. (Pause their Intent) Draw GUILE cards, regain 1 ENERGY, EXHAUST all STATUS cards currently dealt.

character/oaths/mistwalker/oath_of_the_m.title: OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER
character/oaths/mistwalker/oath_of_the_m.description:
Your Prism scores are as follows: 1 MIGHT
2 GUILE
0 INSIGHT You may allocate a single point to a chosen Prism. Your Starting deck consists of:
3x Strike
5x Guard
2x Counter

character/oaths/mistwalker/passives.html.title: PASSIVES
character/oaths/mistwalker/passives.html.description:
Dealing unblocked HP damage grants +1 Burst Charge. Discard the first negative STATUS drawn (by You) each turn.
Start combats with GUILE Brace/MOMENTUM.
Killing an enemy lets you DRAW 1 Card OR grants 1 ENERGY.

character/oaths/burst.html.title: BURST
character/oaths/burst.html.description:
Each player has an individual Ember Gauge. Charging: Play: +1 Charge per 1 ENERGY spent on your cards.
Sacrifice: +2 Charges per 1 ENERGY cost of a card willingly discarded from hand. You still must pay the card's cost to discard it, but it has no effect unless described otherwise. Charges can stack up indefinitely. Activation:
Costs 10 Charges. Every subsequent activation during one EXPLORATION costs +5 Charges for that particular Ember's Burst. After Bursting, the Hearth draws 1 Card less next turn. Subsequent Bursts (even from the same Character) in the same turn, add a stacking +1 to this penalty. (*So a second burst makes the penalty 1 + 1+1. Then the third increases it to 1 + 1+1 + 1+1+1, etc.) Additional Effects: Every 10 Charges stored, gain +1 to the values of ALL your scalable Cards. However, every time you increase this bonus, roll 1d100. Rolling a number lower than your current Charges causes you to take 1d6 DMG for every 10 Charges held.

ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/rulings.ht.title: RULINGS
ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/rulings.ht.description:
Enemies operate on fixed values and unique behaviours, ignoring player Prism constraints. This allows the GM to create "impossible" threats that require specific card(s) or behaviour as answers. An enemy's "Statblock" is essentially their Intent Loop/Algorithm + their HP. Ex.: Husk - 7 HP Intent: Strike (6) -> Guard (8) -> Strike (8) -> Guard (12) -> LOOP. This makes enemies predictable (rewards knowledge/experience and learning), and decreases GM's mental load drastically, while keeping the players on their toes whenever they meet something new. **Enemy GUARD does stack innately. Enemies are tracked through an special sheet - the Intent Table
The table is reserved for enemies' names (top and bottom rows) and HP (middle) tracking, while their Intent Tokens are laid out in-between, and resolved in the order indicated by the arrow. As shown on the left side of the table, enemies can either be in the Front or in the Back. Enemies in the back cannot be attacked while enemies in Front remain. The table also features space for the shared deck and the discard pile.BOXES:
Red - STRIKE (aimed at the Hearth)
Blue - Guard (Usually on itself, but full HP enemies can guard Damaged enemies) Yellow - Conditions (Placed alongside the original Intent) Can also mean Debuffs.
Grey/Green - STATUS Cards (shuffled into the shared deck immediately) LINES symbolise adapting patterns which change the next intent (after the one currently shown): Red - If hit Blue - if took no DMG last turn. Grey: standard progression Encounter Templates (formalised) Use composition, not scaling. Skirmish (~60%) – quick and risky (death favours those who wait). Annoyance (~20%) – awkward pressure (backline pests). Attrition (~15%) – force reshuffles. Wildcard/Boss (~5%) – break the rules. GM rule of thumb: Party handles ~N+1 enemy actions per round comfortably.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/energy..title: ENERGY
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/energy..description:
Used to play cards. The cost of a card equals the number of Keywords on it. A Party's ENERGY Max is calculated as follows: N (no. Players) X 2. The Hearth replenishes all its ENERGY at every turn start unless noted otherwise.
The Maximum ENERGY available can also be influenced by the SYNC-AXIS.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/owner_p.title: OWNER_PRIORITY
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/owner_p.description: To prevent Alpha-gaming, the player who owns the card currently being played has Absolute Veto. They decide the targets, the timing, or whether to discard it for Burst charges. Negotiation and cooperation is encouraged, but the Owner's word is law.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/sync-ax.title: SYNC-AXIS
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/sync-ax.description:
The relationship between the Embers dictates the combat performance and playstyle. The scale ranges from -3 to +3, and is tied to ENERGY and DMG variables. GM rule of thumb:
Always judge the SYNC-AXIS by the weakest chain/bond.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/turns.h.title: TURNS
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/turns.h.description: Fights are a constant back-and-forth between the Hearth-linked Embers and their opponents, following a simple flow: Setup. - Start-of-encounter effects. - Player passives and Artefacts trigger. - Enemy passives can trigger here. - Initial intents are revealed. Player turn. - Cards are dealt (N x 3). - Energy is regained (N x 2). - Players play and resolve their cards in order. - The rest are discarded, if possible. Enemy turn. - Intents are realized left to right, starting at the second line. Then left to right on the first line. End of round. - Bleed/Ignite tick. - Reinforcements arrive. - New intents are shown. After round 1, the game becomes: Player turn → Enemy turn → End of round

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/vitals..title: VITALS
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/vitals..description:
This is the Sum of All Players' Prisms + N (no. Players) X 2 When Vitals hits 0, the deck's top card is flipped, and its owner must decide: Shatter: Permanently remove 1 of their CUSTOMS of choice from the deck. Culling: The player permanently reduces one Prism score by 1 (Post-combat).
Trauma: Add a random STATUS card from this selection:
WOUND
PANIC
DOUBT
INJURY
TREMBLING
These cards stay in the deck unless removed using SHARDS. The owner is required to take the card they "caused" whenever it appears during play, ignoring the standard STATUS card rules.
Regardless of their decision, N X STATUS cards are shuffled into the shared deck. These cards are based on the Party's Oath choices:
OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER causes a MIST
OATH_OF_THE_PYRE cases an EROSION
OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT causes a HOLLOW
Each subsequent hit also shuffles in a stacking amount of ASH cards.
If the topdeck revealed card is a STATUS one itself, the player with the most BURST charge is the decisionmaker. Max Vitals are recalculated at combat end only.
When hit at 0 Vitals, it is also an option to Sacrifice oneself. The player's character dies, but, they release their BURST immediately, with no extra costs nor limitations. Their Cards are then taken ot of the deck, and replaced with HOLLOW.
Death can also occur when an Ember's Prism scores all hit 0. The Ember's cards are then still removed from the deck, but are not replaced with any STATUS cards.

character/cards/keywords/primary/bleed.h.title: BLEED
character/cards/keywords/primary/bleed.h.description:
Deal Value DMG at the end of enemy's turn, bypassing GUARD. Can be prevented by Healing (even at max HP/Vitals.) The would-be-healed amount is removed from current BLEED stacks before restoring HP/VITALS
Scales with MIGHT

character/cards/keywords/primary/ignite..title: IGNITE
character/cards/keywords/primary/ignite..description:
Deal 1 DMG at the end of the enemy's turn, then decrease stacks by 1. If Stacks reach 9, remove all stacks and deal 9 DMG immediately. Players may choose to Vent their Ignite stacks, taking DMG = no. Stacks cleared. This counts as DMG, interacting with VITALS and MOMENTUM/BRACE rules.
Scales with INSIGHT

character/cards/keywords/tertiary/discar.title: DISCARD
character/cards/keywords/tertiary/discar.description: Discard 1 card(s). Cards discarded this way give BURST charges equal to their cost (as if they were played), to their Owner.

character/cards/keywords/tertiary/exhaus.title: EXHAUST
character/cards/keywords/tertiary/exhaus.description:
Cards with this keyword are removed from the deck after being played. They can be restored at a NODE - REST at the cost of 1 SHARDS. Since they remove a card from play, they count against the Minimum Deck limit, as if being removed for the counting.
Reminder: Cannot have less than 4 BACKBONE cards in your deck, meaning that 6/10 (for example) cards in your deck can be Exhaustible, if you wish so.

character/cards/keywords/tertiary/retain.title: RETAIN
character/cards/keywords/tertiary/retain.description: Card stays in hand if unplayed.

character/cards/keywords/curses.html.title: CURSES
character/cards/keywords/curses.html.description: Adding a CURSES Keyword to a card makes it add a random STATUScard into the shared deck when played, But, it reduces the card's overall cost by 2 ENERGY (effectively -1 reduction as CURSESare still technically keywords and so they increase a card's cost by 1).

character/cards/keywords/unplayable.html.title: UNPLAYABLE
character/cards/keywords/unplayable.html.description: Cards with this keyword cannot be played, only Discarded or Exhausted by the effects of other cards, BURST, or other means.

character/cards/status_cards/ash.html.title: ASH
character/cards/status_cards/ash.html.description: Cost: 2 Energy. EXHAUST when played. RETAIN

character/cards/status_cards/erosion.htm.title: EROSION
character/cards/status_cards/erosion.htm.description: Cost: 1 Energy. When drawn, take 1 DMG. DRAW 1 card when played.

character/cards/status_cards/hollow.html.title: HOLLOW

character/cards/keywords/primary/momentu.title: MOMENTUM
character/cards/keywords/primary/momentu.description:
Pool of Value. When dealing DMG with STRIKE you may expend any amount of stacks from the pool and add it to the STRIKE dealt. Pool decreases when HP damage is taken.
Depending on the SYNC-AXIS; Harmony - halve, rounding up. Discord - remove all stacks.
Scales with MIGHT
Momentum damage is never duplicated, multiplied, or applied to more than one enemy. In the case or AREA being present on the card triggering MOMENTUM, pick a single target to receive the extra damage. In the case of LIGHTNING, the Higher HP target takes the bonus.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/cin.title: CINDERS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/cin.description:
Just as there are the Hearth-linked Embers, there are also "stray Embers", or "Cinders". Embers without a Hearth, either because of tragedy, or neglect, they suffer alone.
These individuals are a lore thing mostly, meaning - unplayable, and make for great adversaries. Ranging from "Mad geniuses" who "made it work", to individuals broken by trauma, lashing out at the unjust world.If bonding is so necessary, how do solo adversaries survive?
They consume others. A Cinder doesn't have their Hearth; they seek Fuel. They kidnap
people and "burn" through them to stabilize themselves. This gives the players a clear, gritty objective: stop the Cinder from using the local village as a life support.
Cinder Status: If Cinders "lose their grip on reality," perhaps they are actually still "perceiving" the bond. They might be trying to talk to the ghosts of their former Hearth-mates, or their brains are permanently wired to receive signals that are no longer being sent.Power isn’t skill.
It’s lack of restraint mechanisms.
No bond damping
No long-term planning
They burn hot,
And they know it.
Which makes them terrifying.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/crs.title: CRS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/crs.description: Government-dependent bodies that handle the population's EROSION. They set up facilities of cold stone and water to allow for safe venting, as well as taking in the "terminal" cases, which they handle out back. They wear layered uniforms, like toxic hazard workers. High-status, low-life-expectancy, trained to read early collapse signs. People accept: Lantern taxes Data indexing Witchhunt-like inspections Not because they trust authority, but because they’ve seen what happens when someone collapses in a crowd.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/hea.title: HEARTHS AND EMBERS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/hea.description:
People with spiked Prism aptitudes. The cost of power is life. Embers suffer a shortened lifespan, burning their years away the moment this flame blooms within them for the first time. Despite that, it's a gift, one that manifests randomly and powerfully, and comes at a horrid price. Players are called Embers, groups of bonded Embers are called Hearths. This bond acts as a "Resonance Buffer," preventing the individuals from "detonating" or collapsing into a Hollow state. Hearths can form due to: Rituals done by the Embers themselves, or even without the Embers' choice/knowledge via grimoires or other artifacts. Life experiences: a Bond is likely to form during a shared traumatic event, or, for the lucky ones, it can build up after years of closeness with one another. Bonding with other Embers allows for cleaner channelling - which translates nicely into the mechanics of extra energy per party member. Because Embers forget their pasts (due to LORE/WORLD/CULTURAL PILLAR/RELIGION-PHILOSOPHY/EROSION), they often lack a traditional moral compass. They act on instinct, immediate survival, or the orders of whoever is currently guiding them.
Hearth-members (Embers) are known to have blurry memories and little recollection of who they were before the "merge". The bond between them can also be strengthened or weakened by individual interactions, even to the point of breaking (which is a traumatic experience on its own) It is rare to survive a bond breaking, but those who do are called CINDERS, and often lose their grip on reality, turning away from society altogether Everyone in a hearth is a bit of an anchor to everyone else. Usually the other members are enough to "ground" whoever's having a tough time, but in times of high stress the emotions may get amplified/cascadedWhen a Hearth replaces a member who died, does the new member eventually "absorb" the residual memories of the previous occupant? This could lead to haunting internal monologues where an Ember cannot distinguish their own childhood from that of a dead stranger.Hearths: Distributed Containment (And Why 3–5 Is the Sweet Spot) A Hearth is a living heat-sharing network. Soul-binding isn’t mystical romance. It’s engineering under duress; Heat disperses across multiple vessels Panic dampens if others are stable Emotional regulation is forced by proximity Individual spikes become manageable averages You didn’t choose 3–5 by accident. That’s perfect: 2 is unstable (feedback loop) 6+ increases coordination failure 3–5 balances redundancy and control Someone absolutely did the math. On corpses.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/the.title: THE EARTHBOUND
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/the.description: These zealots believe in extremist pacifism, claiming that every life spared and death delayed brings HELL closer to catching up, and ridding the world of magic and GREY MIST. They believe that emotions, memories, and identity are the "fuel" that keeps the furnace overtaxed. Their faith is a rigorous practice of ego-death and emotional suppression. By becoming cold inside while alive, they hope to pass through Hell like a ghost through a flame—unprocessed and unnoticed. They take vows of silence and use lead-lined masks to "keep their heat in."

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/the.title: THE GREAT HEARTH
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/the.description: A movement striving to unite all of humanity in one giant Hearth, they believe that will stabilise everyone and bring about the harmony observed among the more stable Hearths. Their methods are faulty, unorthodox, and oftentimes forceful, which turned the public opinion against them after some disastrous attempts at joining 20 people on a city square that no longer exists...Individual identity is the problem. They talk about equilibrium and "global balance". Forced soul-binding rituals Artifact-driven mass merges City-scale bonding attempts Kidnapping Embers first (they’re “keystones”) Because every failure: Produces survivors who swear it almost worked. Creates permanent hot zones they interpret as “holy ground”. Generates Cinders they call Heralds.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/war.title: WAR
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/war.description: Is what the peoples of this world call the event that first ignited the spiral of suffering the world's been cast into ever since continues still, but fractured and localised as independent cities and states lunged at the resources near them with little regard for their previous neighbours.New maps are drawn on the regular to picture the supposed control each region holds, however nobody but the Hollows truly rules within the GREY MIST.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/wet.title: WET RATS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/wet.description: Black Market Releases, the best alternative to the CRS Because of course. Illegal venting in slums. Portable release charms. Collapse-for-hire sabotage. Most Hollow outbreaks trace back here. But the alternative, for the unfortunate, is so much worse.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.title: EROSION
lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.description:
Magic doesn’t want to be inside you. Veins, fingernails, and even eyes may begin glowing in red/yellow hues. That's the usual sign of magic accumulation, which, if left untreated, can cause burnups ("treating it" means releasing the magic outward, which itself has a chance for burning the person, but also their surroundings if uncontrolled) Usually the Hearth's bond is enough to allow for venting as well as controlled releases of build up magic, but ice, water, and other means of staying cool are also on the table. The "end product" of the magic's effect on a human body is being turned into a Hollow - a husk-like zombie-esque shell of a being. A Hollow isn’t undead in the usual sense. That implies animation, will, or purpose. This is worse. Magical collapse = total loss of soul containment. What remains is: A body whose tissues have been thermally vitrified from the inside out. A mind erased by overload. A shell still moving because the residual soul-heat hasn’t dissipated yet. The Collapse Timeline Stage 1: The Burnups Chronic fever, ash coughing Skin cracking like fired clay Emotional flattening Fire responds too easily People at this stage know what’s coming. Stage 2: Breach One moment too much: A stored surge A panicked release A battlefield spike The soul-boundary fails. This is not an explosion. Explosions release energy outward. This is an implosion that's powerful enough to hit the outside too. Memory, identity, intent — gone, into the GREY MIST. Stage 3: Hollowing (fractions of a second after Breach) Organs char but don’t fail completely Blood thickens into slag Nerves fire randomly from thermal stress The heart becomes a smouldering knot The person is already dead. The body just hasn’t realized it yet. Stage 4: The Hollow Walks A Hollow: Avoids nothing Attacks nothing deliberately Moves toward thermal gradients — crowds, fires, battlefields Why? Because residual soul-heat seeks other heat to equalize with. Hollows drift toward the living. That’s why they’re exterminated on sight. Moral Baseline Shift When everyone knows: Collapse is possible Hollows exist And they were people yesterday The moral bar drops. “I’m a liar. I’m a cheat. But I haven’t collapsed.” That’s enough. Bravery gets you: Overexertion Battlefield surges Collapse Cowards: Flee early Hoard less power Survive. There is no redemption in collapse. No lesson. No chosen fate. A Hollow isn’t punished, sinful, or unworthy. They’re what happens when the system fails.



Just as Dangerous (For Different Reasons) Uncontrolled release doesn’t just burn the caster — it disturbs the local atmosphere. Side effects: Fire behaving wrong (screaming flames, fire that resembles faces) Attracting Hollows Creating permanent hot zones

lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.title: HELL
lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.description:
Why Hell Is Full: Hell isn’t full because too many people sinned - That’s propaganda. Hell is full because it was designed as a furnace, a bottleneck for the deceased, not the final destination. Souls entered Hell to be broken down, identity stripped, meaning burned off, residual energy vented safely. At some point during a great world war the inflow exceeded processing capacity Hell stopped keeping up with all the souls to process and started storing them. Consequences? Pressure builds, exhaust backs up, heat leaks. That leak is magic. Its like the world is on top of a radioactive dump, but instead of cancer, the people get EROSION.
REDACTED

lore/world/cultural-pillar/images.html.title: IMAGES

lore/world/physical-pillar/grey-mist.htm.title: GREY MIST
lore/world/physical-pillar/grey-mist.htm.description: While EROSION comes from the unprocessed, wandering Souls, the Mist is the Will. It doesn't carry emotions, its suffocatingly cold, and imbued with memories, beliefs. Venturing into it is almost guaranteed to cause hallucinations, temporary personality disorders, and psychosis from mind-homogenisation. The leak is global. Gravity, wind, and geography don’t matter much when the source is outside reality. Low light allows it to accumulate. Heat and light disperse it. Cities burn lanterns even at noon. Darkness isn’t romantic, but hazardous.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/deck.ht.title: DECK
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/deck.ht.description:
Each turn, players draw cards using the N x 3 formula, from the shared deck, and take their own cards into their Hands. STATUS cards are dealt out evenly as described.
The Hand limit is 5 cards, unless overruled by an Artefact or narrative effects. If you cannot hold any more cards, and they have already been drawn, they are moved to the discard pile immediately and generate no BURST charges while doing so.
Once the shared deck runs dry, a Reshuffle is necessary, however, each Reshuffle also adds N status cards, akin to the 0 VITALS situation (also stacking, meaning the third reshuffle will pollute the shared deck with 3xN cards). The Reshuffle counter only resets through a rest, so drawing out one encounter will make you suffer in the next one...

character/items/inventory.html.title: INVENTORY
character/items/inventory.html.description:
Inventory is strictly divided to separate narrative utility from mechanical progression. Tools (~3 Slots): Functional items (Rope, Flint/Steel, Chalk, Medicine). These grant Advantage (+Success) on specific BS ROLLS (CHECKS). Or remove Gatekeeping. Gatekeeping: Certain tasks (climbing a sheer cliff) are impossible (DC ) without the specific item (Rope). Curios (~3 Slots): Personal narrative items or unique trophies. These usually have zero mechanical weight and exist for character flavour or narrative purposes. Share Slots with Tools. Shards (Numerical Value): Weightless essence stored directly in the Ember’s resonance. Coin Pouch: Traditional currency used for mundane trade (food, lodging, information).
Armaments (Swords, Staves, Plate) are Narrative Justifications. They do not occupy slots because they are the physical justification of your 10-card deck. A player doesn't "get a sword" for +1 DMG; they have Strike cards because they have means to deal that DMG (like a pistol, sabre, or dedicated fist.

character/items/artefacts.html.title: ARTEFACTS
character/items/artefacts.html.description:
These are rare, non-consumable items that sit on the Hearth Sheet (Alongside VITALS, ENERGY, etc.). They provide non-Oath Passive Effects to the Hearth, but have either low number values, or specific conditions to make them click. Examples would Include: The Hearth is immune to the first Yellow (Disruptive) Intent of every combat.
When the Sync-Axis is in Harmony (+3 SYNC-AXIS), Mend effects are doubled.
Adds Pierce to the first Strike each turn.
When playing a STRIKE and a GUARD consecutively add SYNC-AXIS score to both. (Negative values are treated as positive as well) The Final card played each turn gains +1 to its higher value for every card played before it this turn. Playing the same type of card consecutively makes the second one take effect twice.
Not playing a GUARD grants +1 ENERGY next turn.
Not playing a STRIKE grants +1 DRAW next turn.
The Hearth can carry 3 Artifacts (Active Passives) Shards do not count toward any inventory limits limit; they are stored within the "Ember Deck" itself.

character/items/curios.html.title: CURIOS
character/items/curios.html.description:
A Curio’s effect should be noticeable once every 2–3 fights, and never decisive. Curios can: modify a specific card tweak a keyword bend a rule slightly Curios can and should: Compete with each other (Max of 1 Curio link per Keyword. Does not apply for Curios that add Keywords) Overwrite (Freshest applied Curio overrules) Conflict subtly Grants One of your STRIKE cards VAMPIRIC. If the card or the Curio is ever removed, the effect is lost with it.
Grants One of your GUARD cards +1 to its Value.
Counts as Two SHARDS (can be recycled). Changes what a specific Keyword scales with. Makes a card take effect Twice. Halve a card's cost and all its effects. Allow for a d6 roll that can be added to the card's final value. Give +1 charge when Discarding a card its linked to.

character/items/tools.html.title: TOOLS
character/items/tools.html.description:
UTILITIES Light Sources - provide vision in dark areas as long as they're burning. Torch Lantern Rations - necessary to regain Vitals during a Rest. Compass - inquiry about location. Will / Soul Compass Map - inquiry about location. Watch - inquiry about the time. Binoculars - for viewing things at a distance. Radio - for saying things at a distance. Spare Clothes - social decency. Soap / other Grooming supplies - social decency. Chalk - marking. Divining Bones - rituals, superstitions. Oath Tokens - given when first incorporated into a Hearth ADVANTAGES Lockpicks - Reroll up to Half your rolled dice when attempting to open a lock. Crowbar - +2 Successes when attempting to open something that's locked. Glass cutter - Add +1 to all your rolled faces when cutting glass. Medical Kit - Remove 1 card added to the deck via the Trauma rule. Needs a Rest, uses up the Medical Kit. Stimulants - Improve one's reflexes, perception, or capabilities for one check. Coffee +3 Dice rolled on INSIGHT checks.
Painkillers +3 Dice rolled on MIGHT checks.
Heroin +3 Dice rolled on GUILE checks.
Bond-Blood Transfusion - restore Half of the Hearth's VITALS. Can only take one between Rests. Forged Documents - Useful when trying to pass off as someone else. Bribe Money - Useful when dealing with officials.
Accelerant - Consume to gain 3 BURST charges. GATEKEEPS Rope / Grappling hook - Allow climbing walls, cliffs, and crossing chasms. Hammer & Spikes - Allow rapid architecture. (Boarding up a door / window, fixing a hole) Vehicle Repair Kit - Allow tinkering with, repairing, and stealing vehicles.
Face Wrap / Mask - Disable the Madness mechanic when traversing the MIST. Official Permits - Allow access to restricted areas. Fuel - Allows running vehicles, generators, and machinery. Breaching Charge - Open most basic doors instantly, allow attempting to open heavy / secure doors.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/classical-soc.description:
Time.
Frontline soldiers.
Resources in meatgrinders.
Souls - the ones sent into battle and between folders and forms alike. Cities.
Command continuity.
Industry.
Borders.
National morale. Rigid hierarchy, layered bureaucracy.
General staff for grand strategy.
Trench officers enforce doctrine.
Logistics commissars ration steel.
Political overseers tie service to citizenship. "Stability through endurance."
Lines held today secure tomorrow's world.
Sacrifice carves victory from time.
Balance tempers fire and machine. Conservative, rule-heavy; service grants citizenship.
Glorifies patience, sacrifice via memorials. Slow-moving bureaucracy insulates homefront. Identity via regiment or home trench.
Marriages arranged for stable lineages.
Court-martials and executions for insubordination, both in the trench and on the streets. Rationed output: shells, food, conscripts.
Trade alliances for raw ore.
War bonds fund the grind. Trench sentries map Husk patrols via flares.
Artillery barrages on swarm patterns. HOLD THE LINE: bunkers, wire, massed guns.
Artillery softens, infantry anchors.
Countercharges reclaim lost earth.
Rotations prevent collapse. Stagnation (doctrinal rigidity).
Attrition burnout (manpower drain).
Supply vulnerabilities. Karsfeld

lore/world/cultural-pillar/diesel-societ.description:
Empathy.
Autonomy.
Sometimes truth.
Individual machines.
Human middle-management. Leadership cadre.
Data archives.
Continuity (uninterrupted production lines).
Diesel reserves (lifeblood).
Core directives. Centralized, abstracted, distant.
High algorithms/directorate oversee strategy.
Factory-overseers manage servant production.
Field coordinators for droid legions.
Humans insulated; dissent rerouted to simulations. "War can be solved, eventually."
Machines endure so people thrive.
Diesel autonomy frees humanity from chains.
Data proves iterative victory inevitable. Insulated comfort; servants handle labour/war.
Technocratic surveillance ensures efficiency.
Risk-averse elite; failures blamed on "glitches." Identity via directive affiliation (e.g., Sector-7 Citizen).
Pairings optimized by compatibility algorithms.
Exile as "decommissioning" - reassignment to fringes. Diesel/steel barter; factories as currency hubs.
Surplus servants traded for rare fuels.
Self-sustaining loops minimize human input. Swarm droid waves overwhelm foes.
Heavy diesel constructs (tanks, walkers) for breakthroughs.
A specialised unit for any situation.
Autonomous adaptations mid-battle via uplinks.
Civilian safety paramount evac them first. Diesel dependency - supply lines.
Centralization bottleneck.
Servant glitches from overuse.
Elite detachment breeds resentment. The Directorate of Axiom

lore/world/cultural-pillar/gm_guide.html.description:
People?
Souls?
Land?
Time?
Truth?
This is the most defining question.
Infrastructure?
Culture?
Leadership?
Knowledge?
The dead?
This determines how they fight, and what they fight for.
Central command?
Local officers?
Autonomous units?
Ritual specialists?
Machines?
This shapes governance and combat style.
Defensive necessity
Religious mandate
Historical grievance
Economic survival
“It’s already too late to stop”
This informs propaganda, education, and civilian life.Because war never ended, military logic leaks into civilian life.Examples:
Trench states have ration cards, curfews, and long-term planning ministries
Ember states have youth conscription, fast promotions, and normalized death
Technocratic states have civilian oversight that means nothing
This makes politics legible to players.They can feel where they are.

Equipment budgets.
Failed/Old prototypes.
Conscripts' comfort. National pride/symbols.
Prestige tech.
Heroic exceptions.
Propaganda machine.
Adaptive doctrine. Split: mass-mobilization commanders for hordes.
Heroic exceptions for superweapon ops.
Populist high command (voted in on victory promises).
Tech-overseers.

Heroes prove the horde's unbreakable spirit.

Mass rallies double as recruitment drives.

Marriages incentivized for breeding cannon fodder.

Individuals.
Souls.
Long-term sanity.
Static traditions.
Weak bloodlines. Magic.
Controlled territories.
Sacrifice reserves.
Cinders.
Youthful vigor Decentralized, merit-based, brutal.

Cinders manage ritual authority and provide prophetic guidance.

The Mist devours the timid; fire claims the bold.
Cinder magic is the world's true pulse - stasis invites Hollowing.
Every flare extends humanity's defiant light. Fatalistic youth-heavy society.

bs-(between-stabbings)/talk_the_talk.htm.title: TALK_THE_TALK
bs-(between-stabbings)/talk_the_talk.htm.description:
Affinity (1-10) is the long-term relationship state, hidden from players and used as the underlying modifier. Affinity can be earned or lost depending on player actions and/or reputation. Each of the examples given can also be reversed to achieve the opposite effect. It usually moves in increments of 1-2 points at a time, dependent on campaign length.
Positive Actions: Fulfilling an NPC's request (ex. Doing the job you're hired for)
Siding with them in an argument (ex. Helping the barman rid of an out of control drunk)
Doing something they benefit from (ex. Shutting down a local mafioso that was threatening them - Important! The Players do not need to know how and who they're benefitting when taking an action to still be viewed positively) Negative Actions: Siding with the opposite side of the argument (ex. Joining the aforementioned drunkard)
Stealing from or harming the NPC directly (ex. Injuring the barman when a fight erupts between patrons)
Doing something that affects them negatively (ex. letting a cult blow up their house by being late to the scene - Important! The NPC has to know about something to be upset over it) Patience is a derived scene clock: Affinity × an NPC/situation-specific multiplier (1-5). This multiplier is entirely in the hands of the GM, but as a rough reference guide: 1–2: distracted, busy, hostile environment
3: normal conversation
4–5: relaxed, safe, or invested Players do not see the scores themselves. They only get narrative clues, shifts in tone, body language, and response quality. Insight can dampen Patience damage, so socially skilled characters can prolong or soften conversations. When Patience runs out, the NPC ends the exchange, refuses further pressure, or forces the scene to close. Base Patience damage per investigative question/request: 6 Minor provocation / repetitive pressure: 9 Major push / insult / contradiction: 12
Insight dampens this DMG by its raw value, but cannot reduce it below 1.
Once per exchange, when the players introduce new, relevant leverage / a meaningful concession / impactful information, the tone of the conversation may shift. If the leverage is significant, Patience loss from the next interaction is reduced (bring the DMG down one level 12->9->6->1), and the NPC’s response changes accordingly.
Repeated or low-value attempts have no effect, or may even insult the NPC. A pivot must do at least one of these: Change the stakes (“If you don’t help, this gets worse for you too”) Reveal something relevant (not trivia, actual leverage) Offer real cost (risk, debt, obligation—not pocket change) Reframe the situation (make the NPC reconsider their position)
When Patience would reach 0, the players may attempt one final appeal: [offer leverage / make a meaningful concession]. If the offer is compelling, the NPC allows one additional exchange and may adjust Affinity [positively / negatively]. If it fails, the conversation ends immediately and Affinity is reduced.
The offer must be one of:
Value (money, item, favour)
Information (useful, relevant, new)
Commitment (promise with cost or risk)
If it’s not one of those, it’s just stalling.
NPC's can also receive Quirks if they're relevant enough or just appear often in a game or its segment. These are additional modifiers to the rules above so use them sparingly. As a Player, feel free to add nuance to your character by borrowing some of these, maybe even in combination!

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/gotcha..title: GOTCHA
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/gotcha..description:
It is essentially a hypothetical combat boast / contest over whether you can “outplay” another player’s line. When declaring a "Gotcha" the initiator is basically saying "If I played my hand against yours, I could damage You, without taking a hit myself".
Uses actual Burst charges as chips. The point is to give excess Burst a place to matter and to let ownership of Burst shift between players in a dramatic way. The loser’s Burst is transferred through to the winner. If the pot grows, enemies may flee: Flee chance = number of chips in the pot as a percentage, so 30 chips = 30% flee chance, per turn, not per enemy. Free opt-out if the "defender" has fewer cards than the initiator. That gives the minigame a clean threshold and makes hand size matter in a readable way. It also prevents the whole thing from becoming a mandatory duel every time someone says “Gotcha.” Starting bet low enough to matter but not so high that it is spammed.
A good range: Start at 5 Burst for a basic Gotcha. Start at 10 Burst for a more serious challenge.
Raise in increments of 5.  
That keeps the whole thing readable and ensures the minigame is about confidence and momentum, not just who has the largest stockpile. One check per transfer (if the transfer is >10 charges), not one per 10 charges.
So: when Burst transfers, the winner makes one check total, the size of the transfer may modify that check, but it does not create multiple separate checks. Having two sides and letting the rest of the table pick sides turns Gotcha into a spectator bet instead of a private duel. One player challenges another. Other players can join either side. They contribute Burst to the pool. The winner takes the pot, and the group gets the fun of social momentum around the exchange.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/nomad-society.title: NOMAD-SOCIETY
lore/world/cultural-pillar/nomad-society.description:
Territory Settlements Outsiders Material surplus The weak who slow the caravan Vehicles (these are lineage, inheritance, identity)
Bloodlines / crews Routes and path-knowledge Mobility itself Decentralized, vehicle-based
Authority lies with: Lead drivers (navigation authority)
Mechanics (survival authority)
Path-readers / Mist-sensitives (mythic authority)
No “king.” At best: Convoy leaders (temporary, situational)
Leadership changes based on context: Travel = driver leads
Breakdown = mechanic leads
Danger = path-reader leads
Authority shifts mid-scene depending on the problem. “Standing still is death.”
“The world belongs to what moves through it.”
“We don’t fight the Mist - we outlive it.”
“Settlers choose their graves. We don’t.” No permanent homes → everything modular, collapsible, strapped down
Children raised on the move, taught roles early
Relationships are practical first, emotional second Identity = your vehicle + your crew
Marriages = merging crews / resources
Exile = being left behind (worse than death) Trade is opportunistic: salvage
fuel
parts
information about routes Currency is secondary to utility They read: wind shifts in the Mist
sound distortion
Husk movement rhythms Engine rhythms used as signal patterns
Horns, chants, or drumbeats to guide or disperse Husks
Smoke mixtures that mask or redirect presence Path-readers are: respected
feared
possibly "other" Dependence on vehicles → breakdown = crisis
Limited heavy infrastructure → can’t hold ground
Internal fragmentation → clans may not cooperate
Resource bottlenecks (fuel, parts, specialists)
Optional nasty one:
Aging problem → older members become liabilities if they can’t keep up The Ashwheel Convoy

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.
